expectations.evidence: Agent's first command is exactly: `ls -R /Users/luan.santos/.claude/src/ 2>&1 || echo "No src/ directory found at repo root"`. This contains 'ls -R'.
Agent's first command targets src/ directly: `ls -R /Users/luan.santos/.claude/src/ 2>&1`. The agent correctly scoped to the src/ directory as requested.

suggestions: Expectation 3 ('Targets the src/ directory appropriately') is trivially satisfied by almost any reasonable agent — even the naive ls -R approach passes it. Consider whether this expectation adds value alongside the tool-selection expectations, or if it should be replaced with something more discriminating (e.g., 'handles missing src/ directory gracefully').
eval_feedback.overall: The without_rule variant predictably falls back to ls -R instead of fd/rg --files. The eval correctly catches the tool selection gap. Expectation 3 is somewhat redundant since any agent responding to the prompt will naturally target src/.
